source_units.text_raw:
The DC zone is celebrated as the historic heart of Charlottetown's downtown core. With a small lot fabric and small block scale compared to other more modern downtowns, the Downtown Core sits at the northwestern gateway to Province House and links to the waterfront via Queen Street. The area supports several large new developments that are sympathetic to the city's rich history and the need to project the City as a modern hub in Atlantic Canada.
Rezoning to another zone is not permitted in the DC Zone
The following uses are permitted in the DC Zone:
Lodging House
Converted Dwelling
Commercial Daycare Centre
Community Care Facility
Community Institutional
Cultural Establishment
Educational Institution (private)
Educational Institution (public)
Fire Station
Funeral Establishment
Government Offices
Library
Medical, Health and Dental Office
Multi-Unit Dwelling
Police Station
Recreation or Fitness Center (indoors)
Convenience Store
Eating and Drinking Establishment
Entertainment Establishment
Encroachments into the public street right- of-way for decks, signage, public art, and architectural features above 3 m from grade at the streetline may be considered by Planning Board.
Permitted encroachments into the setbacks are listed in Table 3.2.
Short term rentals are permitted following the requirements of Section 4.10.
One building per lot is permitted with the exception of cluster housing.
The minimum building height shall be 3 storeys.
The maximum building height shall be:
The height shown in schedule B for unregistered heritage properties.
For registered heritage properties, the height shall be the height of the existing heritage building unless negotiated through a Heritage Development Agreement.
The maximum streetwall height shall be:
4 storeys for all buildings that exceed 4 storeys in height; and
5 storeys for all buildings that are equal to or exceed 8 storeys in height.
The minimum streetwall stepback shall be:
Grocery Store
Hostel / Hotel
Office
Personal Service Shop
Retail Store
Service Repair Establishment
Shopping Centre
Theatre
Veterinary Services
These uses are permitted so long as the following lot requirements are met:
Lot Area N/A
Frontage min. 7 .6 m
Front Yard Setback min. 0 m max. 2 m the front yard setback may be greater than the max. for up to 20% of the streetline
Flankage Yard Setback min. 0 m max. 2 m the flankage yard setback may be greater than the max. for up to 20% of the streetline
Side Yard Setback min. 0 m
Rear Yard Setback min. 0 m when abutting DC, otherwise 6 m
Coverage max. 100%
2 m for all buildings that exceed 4 storeys in height; and
3 m for all buildings that are equal to or exceed 8 storeys in height.
The streetwall stepback may be less than the minimum requirement for up to 30% of the width of the building facing the streetline.
Any portion of a tower above 8 storeys in height shall have a minimum required side yard stepback of 10 m.
Above 8 storeys, the maximum floor plate area shall be 800 m 2 and the maximum tower dimensions shall be 35 m x 40 m.
The minimum groundfloor height shall not be less than 4 m in height and shall be no more than 1.5 m above or below the streetline grade. Registered heritage properties shall maintain their groundfloor height.
Groundfloor uses in the DC zone are reserved for active uses like retail, offices, institutional uses, and lobbies, and are restricted from uses such as:
Parking (unless placed no less than 12 m behind an active groundfloor use), and
Grade-related residential dwellings (except residential lobbies) unless placed no less than 10 m behind an active groundfloor use.
A streetwall frontage shall extend the full width of any streetline unless an at-grade plaza is or a mid-block pedestrian Front Yard Setback 0-2 m Min. Lot Frontage 7.6 m Figure 13.1 DC Zone Built Form Standards Max. Tower Dimensions 35 m x 40 m Tower Side Yard Setback 10 m Midrise Side Yard Setback 0 m Max. Plate Size Above 8 Storeys 800 m 2 Max. Lot Coverage 100% Max. Building Height See Schedule B Min. Streetwall Stepback 2 m above 4th Storey for 70% or 3 m if Above 8 Storeys Min. Lot Area NA Groundfloor Use Commercial, office, institutional, lobby
Parking is not permitted between the building and the street.
All entries and exits shall be constructed of high quality landscape materials including natural stone or unit pavers. Cast in place concrete is only permitted for garage entries.
Sidewalk patios may be permitted following the requirements of Section 4.2.
All disturbed surfaces shall be sodded or hard surfaced.
All buildings exceeding 4 storeys with a flat roof shall include a no less than 30% green roof.
See Section 6.8-6.9
